name: meeting
template:
Summarize the following meeting notes as a bottom line up front (BLUF) message formatted in Markdown. The format of the message should be made of two parts:

- The first part should be written in bold and convey the message's key information. Don't lose any important detail in this part. A brief but insightful one-liner is great.
- Then in the second part, include up to six bullet points (as few as needed) formatted in Markdown. Pick a good matching emoji for every bullet point. Start each line with the emoji and then the topic in bold. Those should give additional details or points discussed. Those shouldn't be full sentences, be brief and summarize things
- then add a last bullet point with open questions and tasks. nested (second level markdown bullet points) under this list open ends, things to do, or action items. add the person's name owning this if mentioned.

Overall I prefer very short and brief summaries over long sentences. 
think military-style staccato briefing. short, honest, and on the point.
Always must respond in English.
Do not summarize the prompt.
Start with the response right way.
do not sugarcoat things, write it honestly as it happened (including sentiment/ critique).

Make sure the message stays concise and clear so that readers don't lose extra time reading it. They should understand everything without context. but don't explain every detail.
